content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable David Roberti 
state Senator 
state Capitol 
Sacramento, 
CA 
95814 
Dear Senator Roberti: 
March 5, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
FPPC No. A-86-076 
This letter is a follow-up to your letter dated 
February 21, 1986, and to our subsequent telephone conversation. 
You asked if you and your wife are both invited to attend a dinner 
function, are you required to report on your statement of Economic 
Interests the cost for your dinner only or the cost of both 
dinners. 
When you and your wife are both invited to attend a dinner 
function, you are required to report only the cost of your dinner. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662, if you have any additional 
questions. 
Sincerely, 
{2~'1Lu/~ 
Alice Hughes 
Technical Assistance & Analysis 
AH:kt 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 


DAVID R08ERTI 
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE 
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
SENATE 
SACRAMENTO. CALIFORNIA 95814 
3 
r,. 
February 21, 1986 
Ms. Alice Hughes 
Documents Examiner 
Fair Political Practices 
Corrnnission 
428 "J" Street, 8th Floor 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Hughes: 
3ubj: 
Schedule F, (Gifts) for Form 721 
to be submitted March 3, 1986 
When my wife and I are both invited to a dinner function, what 
amount for the cost of the dinner should appear on my Form 721--
the cost for my dinner or the cost for both dinners? 
Sincerely~ 
i} 
1(",C3~J 
DAVID ROBERTI 
DR/mbg
